title: Squash & Gorgonzola Quesadilla
summary: A seasonal quesadilla for all the blue cheese lovers out there. It's rich, it's tangy, and it's so, so good.

ingredients.label: 1 Tbsp Chilli Jam, plus extra to serve
ingredients.name: Chilli Jam

method.description: Peel and dice squash, finely chop the onion and finely slice the garlic, red chilli and sage.

Preheat a frying pan over a medium-high heat. Add a goog lug of olive oil and tip in the squash. Season with salt and cook, tossing regularly, for 12-13 mins, until the squash is starting to soften and pick up lots of colour.

At this point, add the onion and cook for another 5 mins. Now add the garlic, chilli and sage and cook for a final 2-3 mins to take the raw edge off of the garlic. Tip the mixture into a bowl, season with salt and pepper and allow to cool a little.

Grab your wraps and lay them out ready to fill. Top each wrap with a handful of grated mozz, a good spoonful of the squash mixture and dot over a little blue cheese. Add a little chilli jam (not too much!) and fold them over to close.
Toast each quesadilla in a dry pan over a medium heat until the exterior is golden brown and the interior is gooey and melted.
Serve with extra chilli jam for dipping.

chefs.name: Ben Lippett
chefs.summary: Ben Lippett (@dinnerbyben) is a cook, food writer, recipe developer, and co-founder of Dr Sting's Hot Honey. Ben spent years working at a spate of restaurants in the UK and Australia – including a brief but rewarding stint at a butcher's – before making the leap to food media.
